question: Will CPI rise more than 0.9% in March 2026?
reasoning: <cite index="4-1,4-7">The BLS report shows the Consumer Price Index for All Urban Consumers (CPI-U) increased 0.3% in March on a seasonally adjusted basis</cite>. This is definitively below the 0.9% threshold required for a YES resolution. <cite index="6-3">The Federal Reserve Bank of Cleveland's Inflation Nowcasting tool now projects the trailing 12-month CPI inflation rate will jump from 2.4% in February to 3.16% in March</cite>, but this refers to year-over-year inflation, not the monthly change. The market should resolve to NO with 100% certainty since the official BLS data shows March CPI increased only 0.3%, well below the 0.9% threshold.
key_factors: Official BLS data released showing 0.3% monthly CPI increase
Market requires >0.9% increase for YES resolution
risks: None - the outcome is already determined by official government data
